hard.next_step: May 28, 2026: Caroline sent Certinal info and RfP template
hard.ais_why: Apr 2026 notes show Rompetrol's Meiramgul preparing a one-page senior-management slide comparing Zycus vs GEP on subscription and implementation cost; AI/agentic capability is not in the buyer narrative. Scope is eInvoicing, eProcurement, iContract, iSource, iSupplier with no Merlin, ANA, Merlin Intake, agentic AI or ML iAnalyze. Formal Evaluation but engagement reads unengaged: 8 meetings with the regional team (Romania pilot then Bulgaria/Moldova/Georgia), and Ariba incumbency drives a price/timeline comparison rather than an AI value case.

north_star_verdict.headline: Meiramgul is an engaged practitioner-champion carrying a price-only Zycus-vs-GEP comparison to an undecided senior management, but her 1 Jul reply was a soft defer ('low start of assessment'), her to-be process map and RFI form are overdue, and no CPO, CEO or the new IT Director has ever joined a call.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 27 Oct 2025 (252 days old). Stage Formal Evaluation since 10 Apr 2026 (no submission-date fields populated). Close 31 Dec 2026 is 178 days out. Last activity 1 Jul 2026 (5 days ago): a genuine two-way email where Meiramgul replied 'on low start of assessment... I will be back to you soon, to share business processes - alternatives'. Buyer-pull cadence held Oct 2025 to Jul 2026, but the champion's own deliverables (to-be process Excel, RFI/edify form promised early-mid June per 28 May) are overdue and the internal senior-management slide has slipped since 14 Apr. The date is far but the internal decision is on hold behind an auto-prolonging 2-year Ariba extension, so momentum, not the calendar, is the problem.
evidence: Buyer re-engaged 1 Jul but deferred: 'We are okay, on low start of assessment... I will be back to you soon, to share some business processes - alternatives' (Meiramgul, 1 Jul email).

Champion-owed deliverables overdue: to-be process Excel and RFI/edify form both promised for early-mid June ('I will send the to-be process in Excel next week... edify form in 2-3 weeks', Meiramgul, 28 May call), not delivered by 1 Jul.
Decision on hold behind the incumbent: 'everything is in hold manner', senior management has not signed the Ariba extension which auto-prolongs for 2 years (Meiramgul, 24 Mar and 14 Apr calls).
No economic buyer engaged: the one-page cost slide is being prepared for the CEO/senior management, but no CPO, CEO or the new IT Director has appeared on any of the 8 calls.

Scope and budget narrowed defensively from ~EUR 450k full-suite to EUR 300k core-four with agentic AI made optional (amount cut 17 Apr; 'compare the basic things we have in Ariba without AI at all', 14 Apr).

metrics.narrative: The business problem is a semi-manual, poorly integrated Ariba/iRequest stack: 'everything is manual... the processes have gaps... it is not integrated' (Meiramgul, 28 May), plus no line of sight from a contract to its invoices ('if a contract has 10 invoices we can't see it... a big gap of matter expense', Daniela Popa and Meiramgul, 24 Mar). The value case is quantified in a joint 5-year ROI model Asmita walked through on 27 Mar against a ~EUR 500M annual indirect spend: conservative savings of ~EUR 6M in year one rising to ~EUR 30M cumulative by year three, with ~EUR 1.6M from the Anna autonomous-negotiation agent alone. Meiramgul's own goal is explicit: 'I want to show the TCO of the system will be lower... savings from direct and indirect budget' (28 May). The numbers are Zycus-built and still need buyer validation (PO and invoice volumes from Daniela), which is why quantifying and locking them is a live move.

economic_buyer.narrative: The commercial decision sits above the champion: Meiramgul is preparing a one-page slide 'for CEO mainly... annual subscription, implementation, and cost per model for Zycus and for GEP' at her director's request (14 Apr), and her director 'asked if we would have the prices' before escalating (24 Mar). The approval chain reads Meiramgul then her director then the CPO then senior management/CEO, and the unsigned Ariba extension is held at that senior-management level ('everything is in hold manner', 24 Mar). No economic buyer is named in Salesforce and none has joined any of the 8 calls, so budget authority is known by role (CEO / senior management, with the CPO gating the technical spec) but is neither named nor engaged. The move is to convert the internal slide into a live in-person readout that puts a Zycus voice in front of that senior management.

decision_criteria.narrative: The criteria are explicit across the spring calls: lower total cost of ownership and faster implementation than SAP Ariba ('SAP Ariba charges a lot... if people implementing 8 years', 28 May); modular phasing with AI kept optional rather than big-bang agentic ('compare the basic things we have in Ariba without AI at all... negotiation agent optional', 14 Apr); SAP ERP integration with payment and invoice status syncing back to SAP (28 May); contract-to-invoice and spend-under-contract visibility (Daniela, 24 Mar); post-go-live KPI/value monitoring ('all the projects need KPIs... we report to management quarterly', Daniela, 27 Mar); and a branded, role-based UI with in-system digital signature (28 May). The decisive lens the committee has chosen, however, is price parity against GEP, which is where our AI differentiation is weakest.

decision_process.narrative: Read from the call sequence, the buyer is running an internal market/alternatives analysis that Meiramgul owns: build a to-be process map, produce a one-page cost comparison of Zycus vs GEP for senior management/CEO, then hold an in-person presentation at Rompetrol's offices before any decision ('I would like you to come here in person to present to our management', 27 Mar). A newly appointed IT Department Director now owns the to-be process and tool direction and was to meet the CPO the week of 3 Jun (28 May), but has never met Zycus. The forcing tension is the incumbent: the Ariba/iRequest extension auto-prolongs for 2 years and is unsigned at senior-management level, so the process can stall into renewal-by-inertia. No mutual close plan or committed decision date exists yet.

paper_process.narrative: This is an early-stage new-business deal with no paper mechanics yet in motion: no MSA, Order Form or SOW drafted, and no legal, procurement channel or InfoSec review opened. The one contracting artifact discussed is in-system e-signature: Caroline sent Certinal information on 28 May and offered a demo/pricing intro via the separate Certinal team, positioned as a cheaper in-platform alternative to the DocuSign the buyer uses today. The relevant forcing function is the incumbent's auto-prolonging 2-year Ariba extension rather than a Zycus contract clock. Visibility would come from co-authoring a mutual close plan once the module scope and pricing are locked with the CPO.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is concrete and owner-attributed. Meiramgul, the procurement-operations owner, describes Ariba/iRequest as manual, gap-ridden and un-integrated and no longer fit for purpose: 'Ariba is difficult to work in... everything is manual... it is not the tool that can be used further' (28 May). Daniela Popa owns a distinct spend-visibility pain: invoices scatter in the ERP and cannot be tied back to their contract, so committed-versus-actual spend is invisible ('we have a big gap of matter expense... we can't see the invoices inside one contract', 24 Mar). Both trace to slow supplier registration and long SAP implementation cycles ('someone's SAP implementation took 8 years', 28 May). Inferred but unconfirmed is executive urgency: senior management's appetite to actually replace the incumbent is still unclear.

champion.narrative: Meiramgul Kusherbayeva, Manager of Procurement, is a developing, genuinely committed champion: she has driven every step since October 2025, explicitly frames Ariba as 'not the tool for the future' (28 May), pushed to add Zycus and GEP into a tender that originally invited only Coupa and Oracle (24 Mar), and is personally building the senior-management business case. What has developed: consistent advocacy, a joint ROI model, and repeated buyer-pull for support. What is still missing: she operates at manager level on a EUR 300k C-level decision, has never introduced Zycus to the CPO, CEO or the new IT Director across 8 months, and her 1 Jul reply was a soft defer signalling a slow, single-threaded push with no executive air cover. Strength developing, at risk.

competition.narrative: The real fight is against the incumbent and inertia, not a single named rival. SAP Ariba (via the current iRequest contract) is the structural threat: colleagues were impressed at SAP's Madrid event and 'want to keep Ariba' and the extension auto-prolongs for 2 years (28 May), so do-nothing is the strongest current threat. GEP (transcribed as 'JEP') is the live peer, and it is the only other vendor on the one-page cost slide for the CEO (14 Apr, AIS note). Coupa and Oracle were in the original tender but have faded since March. We win by moving the frame off price parity: intrinsic AI orchestration (Merlin intake, Anna negotiation, price benchmarking), faster implementation and lower TCO than Ariba, in-system e-signature, and proven Ariba-to-Zycus and GEP-to-Zycus migrations (Heineken, Nippon Gases) that Caroline offered as references.

deal_movement.summary: Live but slowing. The buyer replied 1 Jul (a soft defer, 'low start of assessment'), keeping the deal engaged but drifting through summer. Since March the deal has moved from full-platform ROI modelling to a narrowed, cost-first comparison: amount cut EUR 450k to EUR 300k on 17 Apr, agentic AI pushed to optional, and the decision reduced to a Zycus-vs-GEP one-pager for undecided senior management.

items.change: Buyer re-engaged with a soft defer: 'on low start of assessment... I will be back to you soon to share business processes - alternatives' (Meiramgul).
Amount reduced from EUR 450k to EUR 300k (defensive scope/budget narrowing).
Stage advanced Qualified to Formal Evaluation.
Scope narrowed to core four modules (iSource, iContract, iSupplier, PO/eProcurement) with eInvoicing and agentic AI priced as options.
Caroline sent Certinal e-signature info and the RFP template.
Joint 5-year ROI/business-case model presented and reviewed with the team.

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest current threat is do-nothing / SAP Ariba incumbency inertia, not GEP: colleagues favour keeping Ariba after SAP's Madrid demo and the extension auto-prolongs for 2 years while senior management stays undecided (28 May, 24 Mar). GEP is the live peer and the only named rival on Meiramgul's one-page cost slide for the CEO (14 Apr). Coupa and Oracle were in the original tender but faded after March. Zycus leads on intrinsic AI orchestration, faster implementation and lower TCO, but the committee has framed the choice on price, where our edge is thinnest.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba (incumbent, via iRequest)
competitors.quote: SAP Ariba charges a lot of costs and the result is not what we are expecting... everything is manual... my colleagues are believing them and want to keep Ariba.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the modern S2P replacement: intrinsic AI (Merlin, Anna), touchless automation vs Ariba's semi-manual gaps, 6-9 month implementation vs multi-year SAP cycles, lower TCO, and proven Ariba-to-Zycus migrations (Heineken, Nippon Gases); quantify the cost of staying to break renewal inertia.

competitors.name: Status quo / keep iRequest (do-nothing)
competitors.quote: Everything is in hold manner... it is in the contract under assignment of top management... two more years on Ariba.
competitors.how_we_win: Attack the auto-prolonging 2-year extension with a cost-of-inaction figure and the lost-benefit case Zycus offered ('cost of not switching from Ariba'); give the champion a compelling-event narrative for the senior-management slide.

competitors.name: GEP
competitors.quote: I was requested to make a one-sheet presentation for the CEO showing annual subscription, implementation and per-model cost for Zycus and for GEP.
competitors.source: Avoma call 14 Apr 2026 (Meiramgul Kusherbayeva) + SF AIS_Why__c
competitors.how_we_win: Supply the GEP-vs-Zycus comparison (AI orchestration, time-to-value, Ariba/GEP-migration references incl. Nippon Gases won over GEP) so the CEO slide compares value, not just price.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: They invited Coupa and Oracle for this tender... but they gave Ariba a more affordable budget.
competitors.how_we_win: In the original tender only; not mentioned in pricing, ROI or exec-slide discussions since March. Treat as faded; Zycus and GEP are the live alternatives to Ariba.

competitors.name: Oracle
competitors.quote: They invited Coupa and Oracle for this tender.
competitors.how_we_win: In the original tender only, not raised since March. Assume faded; focus on displacing Ariba and outscoring GEP.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong functional fit on integration, automation and value-monitoring; the exposure is that the committee has reduced the decision to price parity while treating AI, our biggest differentiator, as optional.

items.criterion: Lower TCO and faster implementation than SAP Ariba
items.quote: SAP Ariba is a very long implementation... if they want to change to us it is not going to take two years.

items.criterion: SAP ERP integration with payment and invoice status syncing back to SAP
items.quote: The approved invoice is synced back into your SAP ERP from where the payment can be released... status flows back into SAP.
If a frame contract has 10 invoices we can't see it... a big gap of matter expense.

items.criterion: AI kept optional / modular phasing rather than big-bang agentic
items.quote: We compare the basic things we have in Ariba without AI at all... negotiation agent as optional.
All the projects need KPIs... we monitor them and report to management quarterly.

items.requirement: Provide a full demo / link of the Zycus interface for the basic 4-module scope
items.quote: It will be good to have a link or a full demo of the interface of the Zycus platform... they also asked me to show how it would look like.

items.deliverable: Send Meiramgul the consolidated 3-4 slide pack (Zycus advantages, Ariba-to-Zycus and GEP-win references, cost-of-not-switching) for her senior-management presentation
items.grounding_quote: I will create 3 to 4 summarized slides of our offering... how many customers moved from Ariba to us, GP to us... and consolidate in one email everything for Meiram.

items.deliverable: Return revised pricing for annual license and one-time implementation on the confirmed 4-module scope
items.grounding_quote: We will revise the pricing both for annual license as well as one-time cost, and we'll get back to you.

items.deliverable: Send the to-be process map Excel (by module: objectives, activities, deliverables, KPIs)
items.grounding_quote: I will send you next week the to-be process in Excel... by module, then the objectives, activities, deliverables and KPIs.

items.deliverable: Release the RFI/edify form for Zycus and the other vendors
items.grounding_quote: The edify form will be sent in two weeks or three weeks.

items.deliverable: Confirm the PO and invoice volumes (via Daniela Popa) to finalise pricing
items.grounding_quote: Confirm us the PO and invoice number from Daniela once today... I will confirm you today.

items.quote: The one-page slide is prepared for the CEO / senior management, but no CPO, CEO or director has joined any of the 8 calls.

items.area: New IT Department Director identity + engagement
items.quote: We have a new IT department director... I should make the to-be process.
Everything is in hold manner... waiting for the decision of senior management.
Buyer contacts (Meiramgul, Daniela, Dorina) appear only as task/activity contacts, not as formal contact roles.

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers dominate: break the price-only frame, multi-thread past a single manager, and neutralise the do-nothing/Ariba-renewal option.
flags: Break the cost-only frame: put Merlin/Anna ROI (EUR 1.6M from autonomous negotiation, price benchmarking) into the CEO one-pager so the choice is not Zycus-vs-GEP on price alone, where our AI edge is invisible.
Multi-thread before the readout: get the new IT Director and the CPO into a room; Meiramgul is staffing a EUR 300k C-level decision alone with no executive air cover after 8 months.

Neutralise do-nothing: quantify the cost of staying on Ariba and expose the auto-prolonging 2-year renewal trap, since colleagues favour keeping the incumbent and the extension is unsigned but drifting.

champion_strength.summary: Meiramgul Kusherbayeva is a developing, genuinely committed champion: she has driven every milestone since October 2025, explicitly advocates Zycus over Ariba, added Zycus and GEP into a tender that first excluded them, and is personally building the CEO business case. But she is a procurement manager carrying a EUR 300k C-level decision alone, has never introduced Zycus to the CPO, CEO or the new IT Director, and her 1 Jul reply was a soft defer ('low start of assessment') into a summer lull with her own deliverables overdue. Strong intent, unproven executive influence. Strength developing, at risk.
champion_strength.source: Avoma calls 27 Oct 2025 - 28 May 2026 (4 transcribed, Meiramgul on all); task 1 Jul 2026 email

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Under-positioned. Our biggest differentiator (Merlin/Anna agentic AI) has been relegated to an optional add-on and the committee is comparing Zycus and GEP on price. Meiramgul is personally intrigued by AI orchestration but frames the management case on cost, so the AI value is not reaching the economic buyer.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS is 3.0 Negative: the buyer narrative is a cost/parity comparison with AI made explicitly optional ('compare the basic things we have in Ariba without AI at all', 14 Apr). The calls confirm the field: the practitioner (Meiramgul) is curious about Anna, price benchmarking and Merlin intake, but the buying committee has designed AI out of the decision.

confidence_signals.summary: Engaged but cooling: the buyer still replies and re-initiates, but the pace has slowed, deliverables are overdue, and no more-senior stakeholder has entered after 8 months.
items: Buyer-pull persists: Meiramgul re-initiated on 21 May and again on 1 Jul.
Slowing: 1 Jul was a soft defer ('low start of assessment'); to-be map and RFI form overdue.
Single-thread: engagement stays on one manager-level contact across all calls.

scope_change.from: Full Zycus S2P platform incl. Merlin/agentic AI, ~EUR 450k
scope_change.to: Core four modules (iSource, iContract, iSupplier, PO/eProcurement) with eInvoicing and agentic AI priced as options, EUR 300k
scope_change.detail: Amount cut EUR 450k to EUR 300k on 17 Apr and the buyer explicitly deprioritised AI ('compare the basic things we have in Ariba without AI at all... negotiation agent optional', 14 Apr). A defensive, cost-first narrowing driven by an undecided, budget-sensitive senior management and Ariba renewal pressure.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Meiramgul is a committed practitioner-champion racing us against the incumbent SAP Ariba and GEP, but the deal has narrowed to a price-only slide she is carrying alone to an undecided senior management, with no CPO, CEO or the new IT Director yet engaged.

win_position.text: We sit in a live final comparison against the incumbent SAP Ariba and peer GEP with a genuinely committed champion, Meiramgul Kusherbayeva, who calls Ariba 'not the tool for the future' and has driven every step (1 Jul, 28 May).

The economic decision sits with an undecided senior management and a cost-focused Daniela Popa who flagged our numbers 'very expensive'; no CPO, CEO or the new IT Director has ever joined a call through 1 Jul.

The buyer has reduced the choice to a one-page Zycus-vs-GEP price slide and cut scope and budget from ~EUR 450k to EUR 300k with AI made optional (17 Apr), designing our AI and ROI differentiation out of the decision.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is thinning into summer: Meiramgul's 1 Jul reply was a soft defer ('low start of assessment, I will revert with process alternatives') and her to-be process Excel and RFI form (due early-mid June) are both overdue.
The buyer still pulls: she re-initiated on 21 May asking for support on the 'TO BE' process and the RFP template, and re-engaged again on 1 Jul.
No more-senior buyer has entered the room across 8 months of calls; engagement stays single-threaded on one procurement manager.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer built a joint 5-year ROI model with us (best/conservative cases, ~EUR 1.6M from Anna, ~EUR 30M cumulative by year three, 27 Mar) and is preparing an internal senior-management business case.
Commitment is capped by an unsigned Ariba extension auto-prolonging for 2 years and 'everything in hold manner' (24 Mar), a live do-nothing risk.
No compelling event or committed decision date; the 31 Dec 2026 close reads as a placeholder and the buyer has named only soft, slipping timelines.

deal_risk.text: Do-nothing / incumbent inertia is the primary threat: colleagues were impressed by SAP's Madrid demo and want to keep Ariba while the renewal auto-extends and management stays undecided.
Single-thread on a manager-level champion with no executive air cover into the CPO or CEO who actually approve budget.
AIS is Negative and the AI story is relegated to optional, drawing us onto price against GEP where our differentiation is weakest.

deal_momentum.evidence: 1 Jul soft defer, overdue champion deliverables, summer lull, no seniority rising.

customer_commitment.evidence: Joint ROI model and internal business case underway, but capped by an unsigned auto-prolonging Ariba extension and no committed date.

deal_risk.evidence: Do-nothing/incumbent inertia plus single-thread and no engaged economic buyer.

items.expected_effect: Restarts momentum after the summer lull and pins a date to the stalled internal business case, protecting the 31 Dec close.

items.expected_effect: Reframes a price-only comparison around AI and TCO where we beat GEP and Ariba, lifting win position.

items.expected_effect: De-risks a technical veto and multi-threads beyond a single manager-level contact.

items.expected_effect: Puts a Zycus voice in front of the economic buyer to counter renewal-by-inertia and undecided senior management.

items.action: Quantify the cost of staying on Ariba and the 2-year auto-renewal trap for the management slide.

items.expected_effect: Attacks the do-nothing option, the single biggest threat, before the renewal locks in.

customer_preference.evidence: Meiramgul prefers Zycus and calls Ariba 'not the tool for the future' while asking Zycus-specific next steps (RFP template, Certinal, demo), but the committee frames the choice as a price comparison vs GEP and senior management is undecided (1 Jul, 28 May).

business_case.evidence: A joint 5-year ROI model was built and reviewed (best/conservative, ~EUR 1.6M from Anna, ~EUR 30M cumulative by year three, 27 Mar), but the numbers are Zycus-built and still unvalidated (PO/invoice volumes owed by Daniela), and the buyer's own framing is a cost comparison.
